purpose: The clean intake contract for the daily CRM capture surface. Defines the business fields an operator sees, which are required, the field order/sections, and the technical fields that must never appear in the daily intake path. Split out of config/M365_STAGE_8C_RELATIONSHIP_CRM_OPERATOR_WORKFLOW.json (kept as provenance).

form.title: SharePoint Native CRM Intake
form.principle: Keep the first business-development intake focused on what Adam actually knows at capture time. Who is this, what do they need, is it worth pursuing, what is the next action, what evidence matters. Source-mailbox, message-id, automation, graph, and audit fields are not part of this list.
form.nativeFormAllowedReason: CRM - New Signals carries only clean business fields, so SharePoint's native new/edit form is acceptable. Lists with technical/automation fields are not allowed to use the raw native form in the daily path.

sections.purpose: Operator-facing controls of the Operations Follow-up & Task Backbone (config/followup.contract.json). Optional, opt-in per signal. Reminders = which email alerts (relative to the Follow-up date). Track on = also reflect this on the Calendar / in Planner. The Owner field above determines whose calendar/Planner it lands on. NOT placed on the public Path B website forms (internal concern).

businessFields.purpose: Which intake front-door this signal came through. Operator-visible and operator-selectable. The Path B public brand forms stamp this automatically (Guided AI Labs / Guided AI Journey) via the intake flow; manual captures default to Direct. See docs/CRM_PUBLIC_INTAKE_PATH_B.md.
Email alert offsets for the owner, relative to Follow-up date: 1 day before / 30 minutes before / Day after. Optional. See config/followup.contract.json.
Also reflect this follow-up on the Calendar and/or in Planner. Optional, opt-in. See config/followup.contract.json.

requiredVsCardPlanNote: The CRM / Relationships card plan lists PersonName, PersonEmail, OrganizationName, and FollowUpDueDate under 'Required fields'. The Stage 8C technical contract marks those four as NOT required. RESOLVED 2026-06-22: they stay NOT required (making them mandatory adds capture friction with no payoff).
streamlineDecisionNote: STREAMLINED 2026-06-22 (V5 / Chunk-6 usability finding — the daily intake felt cumbersome). Required set cut from 8 to 2 (Title + NeedSummary) — the only two free-text fields an operator always knows at capture time. The four defaulted Choice fields (SignalType=Referral, IntakeSource=Direct, Priority=Normal, SignalStatus=New) and the two remaining Note fields (SourceText, NextAction) are now OPTIONAL: their column defaults still auto-apply on create, so type/source/priority/status data quality is preserved, while SourceText and NextAction are filled at triage (the next lifecycle stage) instead of being forced at capture. The Chunk-3 verifier (Verify-CrmSharePoint.ps1) only enforces required=True on requiredBusinessFields, so shrinking that set keeps it at 0/0 PASS. Re-apply via scripts/spo/Apply-CrmSharePoint.ps1 (phrase apply-gail-crm-recovery) to push Required=False to the live list.
hiddenFieldRule: Every field in blockedFieldNames must have ShowInNewForm=false AND ShowInEditForm=false on CRM - New Signals. The Stage 8C verifier wrongly let these pass when ShowInNewForm/ShowInEditForm were DefaultTrue; the Chunk 3 verifier must FAIL on DefaultTrue or True.
blockedFieldNamesNote: ItemOwner (Owner) is a legitimate business field shown on the form (Context section) and is intentionally NOT a blocked field, even though docs/CRM_UX_SPEC.md and the card plan list ItemOwner among fields hidden from daily operators. Treat ItemOwner as operator-facing here; it is the assignment field. The first nine names are the original technical/automation fields. The four op_ names are hidden technical keys of the Operations Follow-up & Task Backbone (config/followup.contract.json) -- foreign keys + sync shadow + sync note that the engine reads/writes but the operator never sees. All thirteen must be ShowInNewForm=false AND ShowInEditForm=false on CRM - New Signals. NOTE: op_Reminders and op_TrackOn are backbone columns too but are intentionally OPERATOR-FACING (not blocked) -- they are the opt-in controls.

legacyTechnicalIntake.rule: Must never be the daily CRM intake route. No daily card, button, or navigation link may open Guided AI Labs - Intake Register/NewForm.aspx. It is admin-only fallback (see crm.navigation.json).

publicIntakeAutomation.status: AUTHORIZED 2026-06-20 (Path B) — built in a gated session, NOT by the PnP apply scripts.
publicIntakeAutomation.summary: Two public, anonymous Microsoft Forms (one per brand: Guided AI Labs, Guided AI Journey) feed CRM - New Signals via a create-only Power Automate flow. The flow stamps IntakeSource with the originating brand and writes capture provenance into the existing blocked technical fields, which stay hidden from the daily operator.

provenanceFieldMapping.SourceMailbox: Originating form name (e.g. 'Guided AI Labs intake form')

publicIntakeAutomation.boundary: These blocked fields remain ShowInNewForm=false AND ShowInEditForm=false on CRM - New Signals (the Chunk 3 verifier still FAILs on DefaultTrue/True). Path B does not make any of them operator-visible; it only writes to them from the flow.
publicIntakeAutomation.safetyEnvelope: Public submission is limited to exactly these two Forms. The flow is create-only into CRM - New Signals: no item/list deletes, no updates to other records, no mail sends, no auto-reply to submitters, no external sharing, no guest invites, no permission/consent changes, standard (non-premium) connectors only, no Dynamics/Dataverse. All auto-captured items land in SignalStatus=New for human triage; there is no automated outreach.
